VoP refers to the current Video over Prompts system where learned prompts are used. The shortcoming mentioned is that the learned prompts are shared for all frames, ignoring the order of the frame sequence.
Shared prompts in VoP are shared between all frames at the same relative position in their belonged videos, ignoring the frame sequence.

Position-specific video prompts are introduced to inject information about the relative position of the current input frame. Visual prompts are only allowed to be shared between all frames at the same relative position in their belonged videos.
The position index of the current frame in the video is used to query the table for prompts. The frame position indicates the specific location of a frame in the video.
VoPP, a modification to VoP, increases the capacity for informative videos by introducing more tunable position-specific parameters.
VoPP uses position-specific tokens instead of position-agnostic tokens. Changing only a part of these tokens is found to be sufficient for both effectiveness and efficiency.
VoPC refers to VoP with Context-Specific Video Prompts, where additional context-specific information can be exploited for better performance.
